string.text: A modified version of the original M1 Carbine's 15 round magazine, instead of using its own proprietary .30 Carbine, it uses 5.56x45mm NATO. Also compatible for the M1A1 Carbine, of course.

A modified version of the M1 Carbine's 30 round magazine, instead of using its own proprietary .30 Carbine, it uses 5.56x45mm NATO. Also compatible for the M1A1 Carbine, of course.\n The M1 carbine entered service with a straight 15-round magazine, but the introduction of the M2 carbine in October 1944 brought into service the curved 30-round box magazine. After WWII, the 30-round magazine quickly became the standard magazine for both the M1 and M2 carbines, although the 15-round magazine remained in service until the end of the Vietnam War.
